LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM LEVY'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 1ST AND 2ND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND (FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER) AS TO REMAINING AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES FOR DEFENDANT TO PLEAD WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM LEVY'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Nestor R. Schnasse of Strick Law Offices (721.1200) appearing for plaintiff and moving party
Edgar W. Hawkyard of the Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh (925.825.9500) appearing for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 1st and 2nd affirmative defenses. Demurrer sustained with 20 days leave to amend (from notice of entry of order) as to remaining affirmative defenses for defendant to plead with greater specificity. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797
